roles.judaism: First human, created by God from earth, ancestor of all humanity
roles.christianity: First human, whose fall introduced original sin redeemed by Christ
roles.islam: Adam (Ādam) — first prophet and first human, God's khalifa (vicegerent) on earth

connections.detail: Noah descends from Adam through Seth

roles.judaism: Righteous survivor of the Flood, covenant recipient, ancestor of post-Flood humanity

roles.islam: Nuh — first major rasul (messenger), preached to his people for 950 years

connections.detail: Noah narrative likely derives from or shares source with Mesopotamian flood traditions

roles.mesopotamian_religion: Immortal flood survivor in the Epic of Gilgamesh, granted eternal life by the gods; also known as Ziusudra (Sumerian) and Atrahasis (Akkadian)

connections.detail: Earliest known flood narrative that influenced the biblical Noah story

roles.greek_religion: Son of Prometheus, survived Zeus's flood with wife Pyrrha, repopulated earth by casting stones

connections.detail: Flood survivor who repopulates earth

roles.hinduism: First man and progenitor of humanity, saved from a great flood by Matsya (fish avatar of Vishnu)

connections.detail: Flood survivor and ancestor of post-flood humanity

roles.judaism: Father of the Jewish people, recipient of God's covenant, first patriarch
roles.christianity: Father of faith, model of righteousness through belief, ancestor of Christ
roles.islam: Ibrahim — builder of the Kaaba with Ismail, father of prophets, hanif (monotheist)

connections.detail: Descendant of Abraham through Levi

name: Satan

roles.chinese_folk_religion: God of war, wealth, and righteousness; patron of police, soldiers, merchants, and triads alike
roles.taoism: Holy Ruler Deity Guan; Immortal of Chongning; formally inducted into Taoist pantheon
roles.buddhism: Sangharama Bodhisattva — guardian of temples and the dharma after conversion by Chan master Zhiyi

connections.detail: Serves under the Jade Emperor in Chinese folk religion hierarchy

name: Jade Emperor
dateLabel: Formalized in Taoism c. 9th century CE

roles.taoism: Ruler of Heaven and all realms of existence; sovereign of the celestial bureaucracy
roles.chinese_folk_religion: Supreme deity in folk religious practice; governs the heavenly court

connections.detail: Guan Yu serves in the Jade Emperor's celestial hierarchy

name: Mazu
dateLabel: 960 CE (traditional birth date of Lin Moniang)

roles.chinese_folk_religion: Goddess of the sea, patroness and protector of fishermen and sailors
roles.taoism: Integrated into Taoist pantheon as Empress of Heaven
roles.buddhism: Worshipped alongside Buddhist deities in syncretic temples

connections.detail: Both compassionate female protectors in Chinese religion; sometimes conflated
Granted divine titles by the celestial hierarchy in folk tradition

roles.yoruba_religion: Orisha of iron, war, hunting, and technology; blacksmith deity; clears paths
roles.santeria: Syncretized with Saint Peter (holds keys/iron) or Saint George (warrior)
roles.candomble: Syncretized with Saint George or Saint Anthony
roles.vodou: Ogou — lwa of war, iron, and politics

connections.detail: Fellow orisha; sometimes mythological rivals
Eshu opens the way, Ogun clears the path with his machete

roles.yoruba_religion: Mother orisha of rivers and the ocean; mother of many orishas; protector of women and children
roles.santeria: Syncretized with Our Lady of Regla (Virgin Mary)
roles.candomble: Yemanja — syncretized with Our Lady of the Immaculate Conception or Stella Maris

connections.detail: Identified with Virgin Mary as Our Lady of Regla in Afro-Caribbean traditions
Both river/water goddesses; Oshun associated with sweet water, Yemoja with the sea

name: Shango
dateLabel: Mythological (possibly based on historical 4th Alaafin of Oyo)

roles.yoruba_religion: Orisha of thunder, lightning, fire, and justice; fourth Alaafin (king) of Oyo deified
roles.santeria: Chango — syncretized with Saint Barbara (shared imagery of lightning, red/white colors)
roles.candomble: Xango — syncretized with Saint Jerome or Saint Barbara

connections.detail: Both thunder and lightning deities; warrior gods
Fellow orisha; sometimes depicted as rivals or brothers

roles.yoruba_religion: Orisha of sweet water, love, fertility, beauty, and diplomacy
roles.santeria: Syncretized with Our Lady of Charity (La Caridad del Cobre), patroness of Cuba
roles.candomble: Syncretized with Our Lady of Candles

connections.detail: Identified with Our Lady of Charity in Cuban Santeria
Both water orishas; Oshun is fresh/sweet water, Yemoja is salt water/ocean
Both goddesses of love, beauty, and fertility

roles.yoruba_religion: Orisha of crossroads, communication, and trickery; divine messenger between humans and orishas
roles.santeria: Eleggua — syncretized with Holy Child of Atocha, Saint Anthony of Padua, or Saint Michael
roles.candomble: Exu — syncretized with Saint Anthony or Saint Gabriel; erroneously identified with the Devil by missionaries

connections.detail: Haitian Vodou Papa Legba derives from Eshu/Elegba tradition but is a distinct entity — aged sage vs youthful trickster
Colonial missionaries erroneously identified Eshu with the Devil due to trickster attributes
Both messenger/trickster gods associated with crossroads, communication, and mediation
Both trickster figures, though with very different theological roles

roles.vodou: Lwa of the crossroads, gatekeeper between human and spirit worlds; must be invoked first in any ceremony; depicted as old man with cane

connections.detail: Derives from West African Eshu/Elegba tradition via Fon Legba; distinct in form (aged sage vs youth)
Both intermediaries between worlds at crossroads

name: Loki
dateLabel: Mythological (Norse tradition, attested from 13th century CE texts)

roles.norse_religion: Trickster god, shape-shifter, blood-brother of Odin; father of Fenrir, Jormungandr, and Hel; instigator of Ragnarok

connections.detail: Both trickster figures who disrupt and mediate
Both clever trickster figures
Both trickster figures who challenge divine authority
Often paired as companions and foils in Norse mythology

name: Sun Wukong
dateLabel: Literary tradition (Journey to the West, 1592 CE; earlier legends)

roles.buddhism: Victorious Fighting Buddha after pilgrimage with Xuanzang; tamed by Buddhism
roles.taoism: Great Sage Equal to Heaven who rebelled against the celestial hierarchy
roles.chinese_folk_religion: Monkey King, trickster hero, symbol of rebellion and redemption

connections.detail: Both trickster figures who challenge the gods
Guanyin oversees Sun Wukong's reformation in Journey to the West
Rebelled against the Jade Emperor's authority

roles.akan_religion: Spider trickster, keeper of all stories; outwits more powerful beings through cunning
roles.caribbean_folk_religion: Carried to Caribbean via enslaved Africans; folk hero in Jamaican and other diaspora traditions

connections.detail: Both West African trickster figures
Both trickster figures who use cunning rather than strength
Both tricksters associated with storytelling and cleverness

roles.andean_religion: Earth Mother, goddess of fertility, agriculture, and the space-time continuum; central deity of Andean cosmology

connections.detail: Syncretized with the Virgin Mary in colonial Andean Catholicism; Mary's dress depicted as mountain in colonial art
Pachamama (earth) and Inti (sun) are the primary divine pair in Andean cosmology

roles.andean_religion: Sun god, supreme deity of the Inca Empire, ancestor of the Inca dynasty

connections.detail: Sun and Earth as complementary divine pair
Spanish colonizers encouraged identification of Christ with solar symbolism to facilitate conversion

roles.mesoamerican_religion: Feathered Serpent god of wind, learning, agriculture, and the morning star; associated with priesthood and knowledge

connections.detail: Colonial missionaries and creole writers identified Quetzalcoatl with St. Thomas the Apostle to claim pre-Columbian Christian presence
Fellow major Aztec deity; Quetzalcoatl associated with peace/learning vs Huitzilopochtli with war/sun

roles.christianity: Marian apparition on Tepeyac hill; patroness of Mexico and the Americas
roles.mesoamerican_religion: Tepeyac was sacred to Tonantzin ('Our Revered Mother'), Aztec earth/mother goddess; syncretism occurred at this site

connections.detail: Catholic Marian apparition syncretized with pre-existing Tonantzin worship at Tepeyac
Both represent syncretic fusion of indigenous mother goddess with Virgin Mary

roles.mesoamerican_religion: Aztec god of war and the sun; patron deity of Tenochtitlan; fed by human sacrifice to sustain the sun

connections.detail: Fellow Aztec deity; war god vs knowledge/wind god
Both solar deities and patron gods of their respective empires

roles.mesopotamian_religion: Tiamat — primordial sea dragon of chaos, slain by Marduk to create the world
roles.greek_religion: Typhon — monstrous serpentine giant, last challenge to Zeus's rule of Olympus

connections.detail: Marduk slays Tiamat in the Enuma Elish
Zeus defeats Typhon to secure cosmic rule
Indra slays Vritra to release waters — cognate serpent-slayer myth
Thor battles Jormungandr, the Midgard Serpent
Baal defeats Yam (Sea) in Ugaritic mythology

name: Attis
dateLabel: c. Phrygian tradition (2nd millennium BCE onward)

roles.phrygian_religion: Consort of Cybele, self-castrating vegetation god who dies and is reborn
roles.roman_religion: Mystery cult figure; death and rebirth celebrated at spring festival (March 22-25)

connections.detail: Fellow dying-and-rising vegetation deity
Both undergo death and symbolic resurrection

name: Dionysus
dateLabel: Attested from Mycenaean period (Linear B tablets)

roles.greek_religion: God of wine, ecstasy, fertility, and theater; born twice (from Semele's womb and Zeus's thigh); mystery cult deity

connections.detail: Zagreus/Dionysus torn apart and reborn parallels dying-god tradition
Greeks identified Dionysus with Osiris; Plutarch wrote extensively on this identification
Serapis incorporated Dionysian attributes

roles.slavic_religion: God of the underworld, cattle, earth, waters, magic, and trickery; cosmic opponent of Perun

connections.detail: Fundamental Slavic mythological conflict: sky god Perun vs chthonic Veles
Both trickster/underworld figures opposed to the thunder god

roles.hinduism: Divine twin horsemen, gods of medicine and healing; ride a golden chariot at dawn

connections.detail: Greek Dioscuri (Castor and Pollux) are the Indo-European divine twin cognates
Divine Twins associated with the Dawn in PIE reconstruction

roles.greek_religion: Divine twins Castor and Pollux — sons of Zeus, patrons of sailors; one mortal, one divine
roles.roman_religion: Patron gods of Rome, equestrians, and the Roman cavalry

connections.detail: PIE Divine Twins cognate; both horsemen, healers, associated with dawn

roles.hinduism: Surya — Vedic sun god, driver of the solar chariot, eye of the gods
roles.greek_religion: Helios — Titan god of the sun, drives solar chariot across sky
roles.roman_religion: Sol — sun god, later Sol Invictus (Unconquered Sun)
roles.norse_religion: Sol — goddess who drives the sun chariot, pursued by wolf Skoll

connections.detail: Mithras closely associated with Sol Invictus in Roman religion
Both solar deities of supreme importance in their traditions

roles.native_american_religion: Trickster figure across many Native American traditions (esp. Plains, Southwest, Plateau); culture hero, creator, fool, and transformer

connections.detail: Both trickster figures who create through mischief and disruption
Both animal trickster figures in their respective traditions
Both tricksters who mediate between worlds

roles.zoroastrianism: The Wise Lord, supreme deity, creator of all good things, lord of truth (Asha); in eternal cosmic struggle against Angra Mainyu

connections.detail: Eternal adversary in Zoroastrian cosmic dualism
Created the Amesha Spentas as agents of his divine will
Ahura (= Asura) is God in Zoroastrianism, whereas Asuras are demons in later Hinduism

name: Zoroaster
dateLabel: c. 1500-1000 BCE (debated; some scholars say c. 600 BCE)

roles.zoroastrianism: Prophet who received divine revelation from Ahura Mazda; founder of Zoroastrianism; composed the Gathas

connections.detail: Received revelation directly from Ahura Mazda
Saoshyant will be born from Zoroaster's preserved seed
Both founder-prophets who established monotheistic/dualistic religious systems

roles.islam: Guided one who will appear before the Day of Judgment to restore justice; in Shia Islam, the Twelfth Imam in occultation

connections.detail: Zoroastrian eschatological savior concept may have influenced Mahdi tradition
In Islamic eschatology, Isa (Jesus) returns alongside the Mahdi
Both future savior/renewer figures expected to restore truth

name: Krishna
dateLabel: Mythological (possibly historical figure c. 3000 BCE or later)

roles.hinduism: Eighth avatar of Vishnu, speaker of the Bhagavad Gita, divine lover, supreme personality of Godhead in Vaishnavism

connections.detail: 19th century scholars noted parallels (divine birth, miracles, teachings); most modern scholars see these as superficial
Buddha considered 9th avatar of Vishnu in some Hindu lists, alongside Krishna as 8th

name: Gautama Buddha
dateLabel: c. 563-483 BCE (or c. 480-400 BCE by revised chronology)

roles.buddhism: The Awakened One, founder of Buddhism, achieved nirvana, taught the Four Noble Truths and Eightfold Path
roles.hinduism: Sometimes regarded as the ninth avatar of Vishnu (to lead astray the unrighteous, or to teach compassion)
roles.jainism: Recognized as a contemporary of Mahavira; respected but not central

connections.detail: Some Hindu traditions list Buddha as avatar of Vishnu
Maitreya will be the next Buddha after Shakyamuni
Similarities in ethical teaching noted; some scholars theorize historical contact routes

roles.proto_indo_european: Reconstructed twin horse-riding gods associated with dawn, healing, and rescue at sea

connections.detail: Vedic Ashvins are the direct PIE Divine Twins cognate
Greek Dioscuri derive from the PIE Divine Twins tradition

roles.proto_indo_european: Reconstructed solar deity, personification of the sun

connections.detail: All attested solar deity names (Surya, Helios, Sol, Saule) derive from this PIE root

name: Vishnu
dateLabel: Vedic period onward (prominence from Gupta period c. 4th century CE)

roles.hinduism: Preserver/sustainer of the cosmos in the Trimurti; takes avatars (Rama, Krishna, Buddha) to restore dharma

connections.detail: Krishna is the 8th avatar of Vishnu
Buddha listed as 9th avatar of Vishnu in some traditions

name: Shiva
dateLabel: Possible Indus Valley origins; Vedic Rudra from c. 1500 BCE

roles.hinduism: Destroyer/transformer in the Trimurti; lord of yoga, asceticism, and dance (Nataraja); supreme being in Shaivism

connections.detail: Some scholars note parallels: ecstatic worship, association with mountains, phallic symbolism

roles.baltic_religion: Supreme sky god in Baltic/Lithuanian mythology; cognate with PIE *Deywos

connections.detail: Name derives from PIE *Deywos, related to *Dyeus

roles.roman_religion: Mars — god of war, agriculture, and guardian of Rome; father of Romulus and Remus
roles.greek_religion: Ares — god of war, violence, and bloodlust

connections.detail: Tiwaz/Tyr identified with Mars; Tuesday = dies Martis
Both war/metal deities
